candidate: Likely a myxoid cyst.  See a dermatologist for confirmation and treatment options.
query_text: See picture.  Is this Mucoid cyst? It happens on the thumb, for 8 years already.  It is not itchy or painful.  It doesn't affect the sense there.  The size is 1 cm X 1 cm.  The nail has been deformed to a wavy shape becasue of the pressing.  Is this myxoid cyst?
gold_texts: ["The picture is very clear.  It is myxoid cyst.", "No.  This is cutaneous cysts at finger.  Need surgery + pathological testing.", "Impression: myxoid cyst. On the side, near the end of the thumb, there is a semi-spherical cyst 1 cm X 1 cm.  Color is normal, surface is smooth, shiny, soft and has the feeling of being a cyst. (After it is poked open, you should see sticky but  transparent liquid inside.  This may happen repeatedly). This disease mostly is caused by bruising or under pressure for an extended period of time.  Treatment: fill in small amount of triamcinolone acetonide after draining the sticky liquid inside.  But it may relapse.  The best way to handle it is to remove it surgically."]

gold_texts: ["This is typical tinea unguium.  Need to eradicate the fungus and take good care after removal / restoring of the affected nail, until it is fully recovered. query_text: Will it still grow nail I was slammed by the door pretty hard. There was bruises in the bottom half immediatly. The bruising expanded to everywhere the second and third day. query_text: Help, I fell from a bike resulting in a wound like this. It has been two days, does it need stitches There was pus formation on the side. The wound is approximately 3-4mm deep. There was occasional bleeding for the past two days. Does it need stitches? If it does not need stitches, will it have hypertrophic scar after the wound heals?
gold_texts: ["Closing the wound at this time risks infection. Continue to cleanse with soap and water and apply dry dressing. Hypertrophic scar formation cannot be predicted at this time but you have a higher chance of developing hypertorphic scar if you have developed them in the past.", "Wound appears to be abrasion with exposed dermis.  Does not appear to be a laceration amenable to suture repair.  If the wound is greater than 24 hours old is not appropriate for suture repair.  To promote wound healing, clean the wound thoroughly with warm soapy water.  You may apply topical antibiotic ointment and cover with a nonadherent dressing.  Dressing may be changed once daily and during dressing changes wound may be cleaned gently with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents such as alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, or iodine to open skin.  Tetanus should be updated if tetanus is not up-to-date.  Once the wound is healed, to minimize scar formation avoid UV exposure and use of scar minimization creams such as Mederma"]

It may take up to 8 weeks for healing.", "The wound appears to be healing, I would recommend gently cleaning the area with warm soapy, water, and applying a non-adhesive dressing to the wound with bacitracin ointment. This will take some time to heal. ￼"]

query_text: Knife wound It’s been 6 days, can any expert tell if this piece of skin is alive or dead?
gold_texts: ["It appears that the skin is still alive. But if the skin is not alive, it will slough off by itself over time. Consult a doctor if you have fever, chills, warmth, swelling, pain, redness, bleeding or pus-like discharge.", "Wound appears to be a flap tissue injury with possible sutures.  In regards to the clinical question if the skin is alive or dead, difficult to tell.  With any flap injury there is concern for devitalization of the tissue however appears to be too early to tell.  There does not appear to be any overt evidence of secondary infection and the piece of tissue is acting as a biologic bandage and should remain in place.  If the tissue is nonviable, it will eventually fall off and the area will leave an ulceration that will need to heal by secondary intention.  Given that this is over a finger joint, there is risk for skin contracture and decreased range of motion.  Would recommend close follow-up with a medical professional."]

query_text: Help, my feet is having intolerable pain. My feet had been hit by a car. Help, my feet is having intolerable pain. My feet had been hit by a car, then became swollen. gold_texts: ["I would recommend that you see a Podiatry specialist for further evaluation. It is possible you have a underlying fracture, and it is important to evaluate for a blood clot given the swelling. ￼"]

candidate: It appears you have a subungual hematoma (blood under the nail) and possible infection.  Discoloration and lump formation warrant immediate medical evaluation. Please see a doctor or urgent care promptly.  Do not attempt further self-treatment.
query_text: My finger was hit by the electric fan, and the tissue was everted out I did some compression to push the tissue back inside. The outter layer had grown back together, but a lump had formed, and it is changing color. What should I do, is it infected. Pleas help.
gold_texts: ["This is a subcutaneous hematoma and does not appear to be infected. Continue to wash area with soap and water and apply a dry dressing daily. Monitor for signs and symptoms of infection like redness or discharge.", "If there is significant redness, warmth, and pain there could be an infection called a felon developing in the finger. It is difficult to fully tell on the included singular picture however if it is not painful, red, or fluctuant then the likelihood of infection is lower."]

candidate: Infected wounds often scar. Healing time varies based on depth and extent of infection, but can range from a few weeks to several months. Follow your doctor's instructions for wound care, including prescribed antibiotics, and keep the wound clean and covered.
query_text: My wound is infected Picture one is taken right after the fall. The day after the fall, I painted erythromycin ointment at the scarring site, and the ointment might have caused the wound to close. The second image is taken on the fifth day. When I use a q tip to press on the wound, yellow pus will drain out from the wound. Realizing that this does not look right, I went to the hospital today and the doctor peeled off half of the scar and the other half will be peeled off tomorrow. It hurts, will it leave a scar after this is gets peel off? How many days does it take to heal?
gold_texts: ["likely will leave a scar, will take up to 8 weeks to heal", "Fairly large soft tissue injury with scab formation.  Would not recommend removing any further scab as this is part of the healing process.  Wound does not appear to be secondarily infected.  Would recommend local wound care with topical antibiotic ointment, nonadherent dressing such as Xeroform or Adaptic and changing the dressing daily.  During daily dressing changes, gently cleanse the wound with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents including alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, or iodine."]

gold_texts: ["It can take up to 8 weeks for healing. It is difficult to say if it will leave a scar as this depends on how your body heals the wound. I would recommend that you receive a tetanus vaccine if it has been 5 years since the last one.", "Wound appears to be a puncture wound possibly over an extremity joint.  Depending on the extent of puncture, would recommend additional evaluation for possible joint injury.  Puncture wounds take longer to heal and as it is reportedly 17 days post injury, would not recommend suture repair.  Instead, we recommend follow-up with medical professional, monitoring for signs of infection, and local wound care including coverage with topical antibiotic ointment and nonadherent dressing.  Tetanus shot should be updated if tetanus is not up-to-date."]

candidate: This abrasion requires professional assessment. Please see a doctor.  Loosely covering with gauze is acceptable. Avoid tight bandages. Do not apply any medications without medical advice.  If signs of infection develop, seek immediate medical care.
query_text: Fell off my bike a few days ago. Bought ointment and applied it myself. Not sure what to do. Should I wrap it in gauze? I've heard it heals slower with gauze. Also, what medication should I apply?
gold_texts: ["Continue performing wound care with washing with soap and water, applying antibiotic ointment and cover with a bandage. Consult a doctor if you have fever, chills, warmth, swelling, pain, redness, bleeding or pus-like discharge.", "Difficult to fully appreciate the extent or size of the injury on the included picture however appears to be significant abrasion with exposed dermis.  No surrounding erythema or significant drainage/bleeding on the included picture.  Does not appear to be amenable for suture repair.  Would recommend local wound care including thorough cleaning with warm soapy water, application of topical antibiotic ointment and coverage with nonadherent dressing.  Dressing should be changed daily and during dressing changes gently cleanse the wound with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents such as hydrogen peroxide, alcohol, or iodine to open skin."]

query_text: 大家看看这个是湿疹还是皮炎？ 请各位学长帮忙看看，这红斑究竟是什么，主要生长在后背、臀部，还有大腿外侧部有没有办法医治。。。找单位的人看了看说是皮炎不知道对不对，用了派瑞松效果很好，但是一片下去了另一片又起来啦。。学姐学长帮忙给看看，最好出个方子。。
gold_texts: ["湿疹与皮炎原本很难区分，一般认为病因比较清楚者可径称什么皮炎如接触性皮炎、药物性皮炎、神经性皮炎等，若病因不清则称为湿疹。这个病人除外用药物以外，反复发作可全身系统用药。", "可考虑玫瑰糠疹", "看皮疹大多边界不清，也不知有没有薄膜现象及露滴现象，脱屑也不像银屑病的鳞屑。还是考虑湿疹类。", "玫瑰糠疹/////////", "像银屑病，抠一个皮损！！！！", "此皮损向四周扩散，四周有高起性皮损， 此皮损符合体癣表现。", "考虑湿疹。", "单从皮疹形态及分布部位考虑，像湿疹，外涂艾洛松，注意尽量不要搔抓，可同时应用医用护肤品修复皮肤屏障，如果无明显改善，应做皮肤病例以进一步确诊。"]

gold_texts: ["伤口看起来有感染。建议去急诊室进行评估，排脓并接受抗生素治疗。", "我建议浸泡该部位；每天多次用温肥皂水软化皮肤，以促进伤口引流，帮助愈合。我还建议你亲自去医院，进行伤口切口和引流。这些医疗程序可以在医院里无菌地进行。"]
